title: Python Basics: Error Handling
content_md:
# Error Handling

Programs crash without proper error handling.

```python
try:
    num = int('abc')
except ValueError:
    print('Invalid number')
```

## Common Exceptions
| Exception | Meaning |
|-----------|---------|
| `ValueError` | Wrong value type |
| `ZeroDivisionError` | Divide by zero |
| `TypeError` | Wrong data type |

ML pipelines depend heavily on error handling.

questions.question: Write a try/except block catching ZeroDivisionError.
Why use exceptions?
questions.answer: To avoid crashes and handle issues gracefully.

questions.question: Which exception happens when converting text to int incorrectly?
